TSLA: How Autocallable ETFs Are Revolutionizing Single-Stock Income. GraniteShares is introducing autocallable single-stock ETFs to make institutional income strategies more accessible to advisors and investors. These ETFs aim to overcome traditional structured note drawbacks like illiquidity and high fees by offering continuous liquidity, lower costs, and a laddered design. The strategy, which combines elements of equities and fixed income, provides defined income streams and downside protection, leveraging the volatility of high-growth stocks like Tesla and Nvidia.
TGT: How Target-Heavy Balanced Funds Could Magnify Systemic Risk Amid Retail Sector Volatility. Target Corp (TGT) has disclosed a new risk related to balanced funds that heavily weight the company's stock. These funds, by including Target alongside bonds and other equities, could amplify systemic risk if the retail sector experiences a sharp decline in sentiment, potentially forcing broad portfolio rebalancing and depressing Target's stock price. The average price target for TGT is $126.04, suggesting an 8.89% upside potential.

Amazon plans to move Prime Day event to June from July, Bloomberg News reports. Amazon is reportedly planning to shift its annual Prime Day sales event from July to late June, according to Bloomberg News. This change would move the critical shopping event, known for significant discounts, into Amazon's second fiscal quarter. The move could also help Amazon compete more effectively with similar sales events offered by rivals like Walmart and Target.
SBUX: Bristol John W & Co. Inc. NY Purchases 353,751 Shares of Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. Bristol John W & Co. Inc. NY has significantly increased its stake in Starbucks Corporation ($SBUX) by purchasing 353,751 shares, bringing its total holdings to over 1.3 million shares valued at approximately $113.7 million. This makes Starbucks the firm's 20th-largest holding. The article also details Starbucks' recent earnings, institutional and insider trading activity, and current analyst ratings, which reflect a "Moderate Buy" consensus despite mixed sentiment regarding the company's future performance.

Why Josh Brown sees Starbucks as 'best stock in the market'. Veteran trader Josh Brown considers Starbucks (SBUX) the "best stock in the market" due to its indispensable product, high-stakes leadership turnaround under Brian Niccol, and a recently repaired technical chart. He argues that Starbucks offers an "affordable luxury" and its "Back to Starbucks" strategy is driving revenue reacceleration and margin expansion. Technically, the stock is breaking out, having reclaimed its 200-day moving average and pushing past the $100 pivot level, with a $120 price objective as long as it stays above its 50-day MA.

Why Josh Brown sees Starbucks as 'best stock in the market'. Josh Brown, CEO of Ritholtz Wealth Management, considers Starbucks (SBUX) the "best stock in the market" despite its past underperformance. He attributes his bullish outlook to the company's resilient demand for its "affordable luxury" product, the strategic leadership turnaround by CEO Brian Niccol, and a strong technical breakout on its stock chart. Brown believes these factors position Starbucks for a return to all-time highs, setting a price objective of $120.

Starbucks Stock Performance & Business Trends Analysis 2026 - News and Statistics. Starbucks stock has shown strong returns over the past six months, outperforming the market with a price of $100.49 per share. However, underlying business concerns include average annual declines in same-store sales over two years, a substantial drop in operating margin to 7.2%, and decreasing earnings per share over six years despite revenue growth. The analysis suggests that the stock's current high valuation already reflects much positive news and that other companies may offer stronger fundamental business characteristics.
DIS: The Walt Disney Company $DIS Holdings Trimmed by Entropy Technologies LP. Entropy Technologies LP reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) by 25.3% in the third quarter, selling over 26,000 shares. Despite this reduction, Walt Disney remains a significant holding for the firm, representing 0.7% of its portfolio. Various other institutional investors have also adjusted their positions in DIS, while analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $135.80.

Evercore ISI cuts Webtoon Entertainment stock price target on soft outlook. Evercore ISI has cut its price target for Webtoon Entertainment (WBTN) to $15 from $20, while maintaining an Outperform rating due to inconsistent execution and a soft first-quarter outlook. The firm lowered its revenue and adjusted EBITDA estimates for fiscal years 2026 and 2027. Webtoon aims to achieve double-digit revenue growth by Q4 2026 through infrastructure projects, advertising recovery, and IP growth, including a new Webtoon-Disney platform.

Paul Roeder Named Chief Communications Officer of The Walt Disney Company. Paul Roeder has been appointed as the Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Communications Officer of The Walt Disney Company, effective March 19, reporting directly to incoming CEO Josh D’Amaro. A 25-year Disney veteran, Roeder will oversee Disney's worldwide communications and public relations strategy. He previously led communications for Disney Entertainment Studios, Direct-to-Consumer, and International, playing a key role in major studio acquisitions and the launch of Disney+.

Paul Roeder Ascends to Chief Communications Officer of The Walt Disney Company. Paul Roeder, a 25-year veteran at Disney, has been appointed as the Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Communications Officer for The Walt Disney Company, effective March 19. He will report directly to incoming CEO Josh D’Amaro, overseeing all global communications and public relations strategies. Roeder's extensive experience includes communications leadership during major acquisitions like Lucasfilm and 21st Century Fox, and overseeing communications for the launch of Disney+ and numerous blockbuster film campaigns.

JetBlue and Disneyland® Paris Bring the World of Frozen to the Skies with a Royal Sweepstakes. JetBlue has partnered with Disneyland Paris to celebrate the grand opening of the park’s World of Frozen on March 29, 2026. This collaboration includes a sweepstakes offering one group of four a VIP trip to Disneyland Paris, featuring round-trip travel in JetBlue's Mint experience and exclusive park benefits. The partnership also highlights JetBlue’s transatlantic service to Paris and inflight entertainment with Frozen movies.

Paul Roeder Named Chief Communications Officer of The Walt Disney Company. Paul Roeder has been appointed as the Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Communications Officer of The Walt Disney Company, effective March 19. With 25 years of experience at Disney, Roeder will be responsible for leading the company's global communications strategy, reporting directly to CEO Josh D’Amaro. His role includes overseeing various communications functions, media relations, and corporate social responsibility across Disney’s worldwide operations.

Disney appoints veteran Paul Roeder as communications chief. Walt Disney Co. has appointed Paul Roeder as its new senior executive vice president and chief communications officer, effective March 19. Roeder, a 25-year company veteran, will oversee worldwide communications and serve as Disney's lead spokesperson, reporting to incoming chief executive Josh D'Amaro. This appointment is one of several leadership changes following D'Amaro's recent designation as the new top boss.

Disney hands global communications reins to 25-year insider. The Walt Disney Company has appointed Paul Roeder as its new Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Communications Officer, effective March 19. A 25-year Disney veteran, Roeder will lead the company's worldwide communications and public relations strategy, reporting directly to incoming CEO Josh D’Amaro. He previously oversaw communications for Disney Entertainment Studios, Direct-to-Consumer, and International, playing a key role in major acquisitions and film releases.

Incoming Disney CEO Josh D’Amaro Picks Company Vet Paul Roeder To Lead Corp Comms. Incoming Disney CEO Josh D’Amaro has promoted 25-year company veteran Paul Roeder to Senior EVP and Chief Communications Officer. Roeder will lead Disney's global communications and public relations, serving as the company's lead spokesperson, with his promotion effective March 19. He has played a pivotal role in major Disney acquisitions and launches, including the integration of 21st Century Fox and the launch of Disney+.

More ‘Bluey’ Is Coming to Disney+. Disney+ is expanding its "Bluey" content with new additions in March and May 2026. This includes "Bluey’s Big Play – The Stage Show," a televised version of the popular touring production, and a collection of "Bluey Minisodes" previously available only online and in Australia. The global phenomenon "Bluey" was the top-streamed program in the U.S. for the second consecutive year in 2025.

The Walt Disney Company (DIS) Sees Strong U.S. Debut from New Pixar Film. The Walt Disney Company (DIS) saw a strong U.S. debut for its new Pixar film, "Hoppers," which grossed an estimated $46 million and marks the best launch for new Pixar characters since "Coco" in 2017. This success highlights Pixar's continued ability to attract audiences with original stories. Additionally, the article notes the upcoming departure of Kristina Schake, Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Communications Officer, aligning with Bob Iger’s CEO tenure conclusion.
NKE: Nike Names Cimarron Nix CSO As Sustainability Becomes Earnings Risk Focus. Nike has appointed Cimarron Nix as its new Chief Sustainability Officer, a move aimed at addressing regulatory and reputational pressures around sustainability and labor practices. Nix's extensive background in sustainable manufacturing and labor issues is expected to integrate ESG priorities more closely with Nike's operational and strategic decisions, potentially impacting supply chain standards and regulatory engagement. This appointment comes as Nike faces declining stock performance and increased scrutiny, making sustainability a key factor in earnings quality and investor confidence.

American Airlines makes AAdvantage miles more flexible with ability to redeem miles for gift cards. American Airlines has enhanced its AAdvantage program by allowing members to redeem miles for gift cards from various top brands, including Sephora, Nike, and Best Buy. This new platform, aadvantagegiftcards.com, offers gift cards ranging from $10 to $500, providing more everyday redemption options for AAdvantage members. This initiative aims to offer greater flexibility and value for members' miles, building on previous expansions like AAdvantage Exchange and AAdvantage Cruises.
